{'text': '愛知県丹羽郡扶桑町山那', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:山那神社 (扶桑町山那)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929641}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yana Shrine | 画像= [[File:Yana Jinja（Yana）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}{{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}} Ōaza Yana, Aza Yashikichi 609 | 位置={{coord|35|22|22.42|N|136|54|43.11|E|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Yana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yana Shrine''' (やなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yana|Q106705806|WD=Q106705806|1=Yamana, Fuso}}, {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown. It is said that this shrine is the inner-shrine Yana Shrine in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' and “Junior Third Rank Yana Tenjin” in the provincial register. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was ranked village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), Tenjin-sha was renamed Yana Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) it received official-offering designation. On {{ill|lt=November 13|Q2984|WD=Q2984|1=November 13}}, {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44), Aoki-sha in Aza Ōmochi was merged. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, this shrine stood on a midstream island in the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, but in the great flood of {{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|WD=Q6725|1=1601}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6), houses, temples/shrines, and fields were all washed away, so the shrine was moved into the precinct of the subsidiary Shinmei-sha south of the levee. During that flood, the main shrine image drifted as far as Kōmyōji village in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} (present {{ill|lt=Kōmyōji (place name in Ichinomiya)|Q127442823|WD=Q127442823|lt=Kōmyōji|1=Kōmyoji}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}), where villagers recovered it and worshiped it in the precinct of the Tendai temple Kōmyōji. Villagers of Yana repeatedly sought its return, and it is said to have been returned on February 8, {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2). This gave rise to the legend of “Kōmyōji-nagare.” === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Site of Aoki Shrine === West of Ryūsen-ji in Yana there was Aoki Shrine enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto. During {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinbutsu-konko|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ryūsen-ji and Aoki Shrine are thought to have been one body; in {{ill|lt=1820|Q6973|WD=Q6973|1=1820}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 3), it separated from the temple and was reportedly restored as Aoki Shrine at the time of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}. On November 13, 1911, it was merged into Yana Shrine. The former site is now Yamana Nursery School, with a stone marker in one corner. Place names such as Aoki and Aoki-mae remain nearby. === Site of Tenjin Shrine === Near the old shrine site are place names such as Ōtorii-guchi, Miyuki-michi, Magusa-yama, Tenjin-yama, and Tenjin-mae, thought to date from when Tenjin Shrine was enshrined. {{See also|北山名村}} === Location === * 609 Yashikichi, Ōaza Yana, Niwa District, Fusō, Aichi Prefecture **About 900 m northeast of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Yamana Elementary School|Q81976214|WD=Q81976214|1=Fusō Municipal Yamana Elementary School}}. South of the {{ill|lt=Migoi levee|Q11488847|WD=Q11488847|1=Migoi levee}} on the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}. === Transportation === *About 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kizuyōsui Station|Q6434256|WD=Q6434256|1=Kotsuyōsui Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}. *About 45 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the same line. === References === *''History of Fusō Town'', {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=1998|Q2089|WD=Q2089|1=1998}}, chapter 2 Religion, p.504. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|Q11465292|WD=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} * {{ill|lt=Yana Shrine (Minamiyamana, Fusō)|Q11471014|WD=Q11471014|1=Yana Shrine (Q11471014)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山那]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

During that flood, the main shrine image drifted as far as Kōmyōji village in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} (present {{ill|lt=Kōmyōji (place name in Ichinomiya)|Q127442823|WD=Q127442823|lt=Kōmyōji|1=Kōmyoji}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}), where villagers recovered it and worshiped it in the precinct of the Tendai temple Kōmyōji. Villagers of Yana repeatedly sought its return, and it is said to have been returned on February 8, {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2). This gave rise to the legend of “Kōmyōji-nagare.” === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Site of Aoki Shrine === West of Ryūsen-ji in Yana there was Aoki Shrine enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto. During {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinbutsu-konko|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ryūsen-ji and Aoki Shrine are thought to have been one body; in {{ill|lt=1820|Q6973|WD=Q6973|1=1820}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 3), it separated from the temple and was reportedly restored as Aoki Shrine at the time of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}. On November 13, 1911, it was merged into Yana Shrine. The former site is now Yamana Nursery School, with a stone marker in one corner. Place names such as Aoki and Aoki-mae remain nearby. === Site of Tenjin Shrine === Near the old shrine site are place names such as Ōtorii-guchi, Miyuki-michi, Magusa-yama, Tenjin-yama, and Tenjin-mae, thought to date from when Tenjin Shrine was enshrined. {{See also|北山名村}} === Location === * 609 Yashikichi, Ōaza Yana, Niwa District, Fusō, Aichi Prefecture **About 900 m northeast of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Yamana Elementary School|Q81976214|WD=Q81976214|1=Fusō Municipal Yamana Elementary School}}. South of the {{ill|lt=Migoi levee|Q11488847|WD=Q11488847|1=Migoi levee}} on the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}. === Transportation === *About 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kizuyōsui Station|Q6434256|WD=Q6434256|1=Kotsuyōsui Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}. *About 45 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the same line. === References === *''History of Fusō Town'', {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=1998|Q2089|WD=Q2089|1=1998}}, chapter 2 Religion, p.504. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|Q11465292|WD=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} * {{ill|lt=Yana Shrine (Minamiyamana, Fusō)|Q11471014|WD=Q11471014|1=Yana Shrine (Q11471014)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:山那]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]

{'text': '愛知県丹羽郡扶桑町山那', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:山那神社 (扶桑町山那)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929641}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yana Shrine | 画像= [[File:Yana Jinja（Yana）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}{{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}} Ōaza Yana, Aza Yashikichi 609 | 位置={{coord|35|22|22.42|N|136|54|43.11|E|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格=former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Yana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yana Shrine''' (やなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yana|Q106705806|WD=Q106705806|1=Yamana, Fuso}}, {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown. It is said that this shrine is the inner-shrine Yana Shrine in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' and “Junior Third Rank Yana Tenjin” in the provincial register. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was ranked village shrine under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=1897|Q7847|WD=Q7847|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), Tenjin-sha was renamed Yana Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) it received official-offering designation. On {{ill|lt=November 13|Q2984|WD=Q2984|1=November 13}}, {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44), Aoki-sha in Aza Ōmochi was merged. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, this shrine stood on a midstream island in the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, but in the great flood of {{ill|lt=1601|Q6725|WD=Q6725|1=1601}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 6), houses, temples/shrines, and fields were all washed away, so the shrine was moved into the precinct of the subsidiary Shinmei-sha south of the levee. During that flood, the main shrine image drifted as far as Kōmyōji village in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} (present {{ill|lt=Kōmyōji (place name in Ichinomiya)|Q127442823|WD=Q127442823|lt=Kōmyōji|1=Kōmyoji}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}), where villagers recovered it and worshiped it in the precinct of the Tendai temple Kōmyōji. Villagers of Yana repeatedly sought its return, and it is said to have been returned on February 8, {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2). This gave rise to the legend of “Kōmyōji-nagare.” === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Site of Aoki Shrine === West of Ryūsen-ji in Yana there was Aoki Shrine enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto. During {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shinbutsu-konko|1=UNKNOWN}}, Ryūsen-ji and Aoki Shrine are thought to have been one body; in {{ill|lt=1820|Q6973|WD=Q6973|1=1820}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 3), it separated from the temple and was reportedly restored as Aoki Shrine at the time of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}. On November 13, 1911, it was merged into Yana Shrine. The former site is now Yamana Nursery School, with a stone marker in one corner. Place names such as Aoki and Aoki-mae remain nearby. === Site of Tenjin Shrine === Near the old shrine site are place names such as Ōtorii-guchi, Miyuki-michi, Magusa-yama, Tenjin-yama, and Tenjin-mae, thought to date from when Tenjin Shrine was enshrined. {{See also|北山名村}} === Location === * 609 Yashikichi, Ōaza Yana, Niwa District, Fusō, Aichi Prefecture **About 900 m northeast of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Yamana Elementary School|Q81976214|WD=Q81976214|1=Fusō Municipal Yamana Elementary School}}. South of the {{ill|lt=Migoi levee|Q11488847|WD=Q11488847|1=Migoi levee}} on the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|WD=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}. === Transportation === *About 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kizuyōsui Station|Q6434256|WD=Q6434256|1=Kotsuyōsui Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}}. *About 45 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the same line. === References === *''History of Fusō Town'', {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=1998|Q2089|WD=Q2089|1=1998}}, chapter 2 Religion, p.504. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|Q11465292|WD=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} * {{ill|lt=Yana Shrine (Minamiyamana, Fusō)|Q11471014|WD=Q11471014|1=Yana Shrine (Q11471014)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:山那]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
